rationale: The cited paper is used in the methods framing as part of the core Bayesian conjoint design literature, not just in a peripheral literature dump. The citing paper explicitly positions its new f-criterion against the traditional Bayesian D-criterion approach and cites Sandor and Wedel (2001) as a representative prior-belief Bayesian design reference in that setup. It also points to Sandor and Wedel (2001) when discussing how prior means/variances can be informed by managers' beliefs. This indicates a meaningful methodological grounding role for the cited work.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Sandor and Wedel (2001) as methodological precedent for Bayesian conjoint choice design using prior beliefs. It builds on that single-objective Bayesian D-design tradition when introducing and motivating its new dual-objective f-criterion framework.
